_meta.description: CBO-scored legislation used for fiscal accountability tracking. All deficit impacts are 10-year estimates relative to the CBO baseline at time of scoring. Positive = adds to deficit. Negative = reduces deficit.
_meta.scoring_convention: cbo_10yr_b: positive = adds to deficit (costs), negative = reduces deficit (saves). Figures are billions of dollars.
_meta.methodology: Each member's fiscal record is computed by summing CBO-scored impact of bills they voted Yea on. Voting Nay on a deficit-reducing bill is shown separately. No editorial weighting is applied — this is arithmetic on official government scores.

sources.label: CBO: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (Aug 2021)
CBO: One Big Beautiful Bill Act (Jul 2025)

votes.name: Tax Cuts and Jobs Act
votes.cbo_note: CBO/JCT scored the conference agreement at +$1,455B over 2018–2027. Including debt-service costs: ~$1,800B total impact.
votes.description: Permanently cut the corporate tax rate from 35% to 21%; temporarily reduced individual income tax rates (expiring 2025); doubled the standard deduction.
votes.partisan_pattern: Near party-line: 51R yea / 48D-I nay in Senate; 224R yea / 201D nay in House

votes.name: Bipartisan Budget Act of 2018
votes.cbo_note: CBO scored +$320B over 10 years. Lifted Budget Control Act discretionary spending caps by $296B over FY2018–2019.
votes.description: Suspended the debt ceiling through March 2019 and raised discretionary spending caps by $296 billion over two years for both defense and non-defense programs.
votes.partisan_pattern: Bipartisan in Senate (71-28); mixed in House (167R+73D yea vs 67R+119D nay)

votes.name: CARES Act
votes.cbo_note: CBO/JCT estimated +~$1.7T over 2020–2030. Emergency COVID-19 response.
votes.description: Emergency pandemic relief: $1,200 direct payments, $600B+ in small business loans (PPP), expanded unemployment insurance, and $175B for hospitals and healthcare.
votes.house_vote_note: House passed by voice vote on March 27, 2020 — no recorded roll call exists.
votes.partisan_pattern: Unanimous in Senate (96-0). House: voice vote, no roll call.

votes.name: American Rescue Plan Act
votes.cbo_note: CBO scored +$1.9T over 2021–2031. Nearly all costs front-loaded in FY2021–2022.
votes.description: Pandemic relief: $1,400 direct payments, expanded child tax credit, $350B in state/local aid, vaccine distribution, and extended supplemental unemployment benefits.
votes.partisan_pattern: Near party-line: 50D-I yea / 49R nay in Senate; 220D yea / 211R+1D nay in House

votes.name: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act
votes.cbo_note: CBO scored +$256B net over 2021–2031. Includes $415B in new discretionary spending offset by $110B in mandatory savings and $50B in new revenues.
votes.description: Authorized $1.2 trillion for roads, bridges, broadband, rail, water systems, and electric vehicle infrastructure. Net deficit impact $256B after mandatory offsets.
votes.partisan_pattern: Bipartisan in Senate (50D+19R yea); mostly D in House (215D+13R yea vs 200R+6D nay)

votes.name: Inflation Reduction Act
votes.cbo_note: CBO/JCT scored approximately -$305B net deficit reduction over 2022–2031. Revenue: +$739B (drug pricing, minimum corporate tax, IRS enforcement). Spending: +$433B (climate credits, ACA extensions). Net: ~-$305B.
votes.description: Largest U.S. climate investment in history ($369B in clean energy credits) plus Medicare drug pricing reform; financed by a 15% minimum corporate tax, IRS enforcement funding, and other revenue measures.
votes.partisan_pattern: Party-line: 51D yea (inc. VP Harris tiebreak) / 50R nay in Senate; 220D yea / 207R nay in House

votes.name: Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023
votes.cbo_note: CBO projected -$1.5T over 2023–2033. IMPORTANT: ~$1.49T of savings are from discretionary spending caps that future Congresses must honor in annual appropriations. Hard mandatory/revenue savings: ~$10B. The cap savings are projections, not guaranteed.
votes.description: Debt ceiling suspension through January 2025 paired with discretionary spending caps for FY2024–2025, limits on unspent COVID funds, and mandatory program savings. CBO projected $1.5T in deficit reduction, primarily from the spending caps.
votes.partisan_pattern: Bipartisan: 149R+165D yea vs 71R+46D nay in House; 17R+46D-I yea in Senate

votes.name: One Big Beautiful Bill Act
votes.cbo_note: CBO scored +$3.4T over 2025–2034 (vs. CBO Jan 2025 baseline). Breakdown: -$1.1T mandatory cuts (Medicaid, SNAP, student loans) offset by -$4.5T in revenue reductions (TCJA permanence + new cuts). Including debt service: ~$4.1T total impact.
votes.description: Made 2017 TCJA tax cuts permanent and added new reductions (no tax on tips/overtime, expanded deductions) while cutting Medicaid, SNAP, and student loan programs. CBO's largest single-bill deficit score in recent history.
votes.partisan_pattern: Near party-line: 50R yea + VP Vance / 50D-I nay in Senate; ~216R+2D yea vs all D + ~2R nay in House
